item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
+ spoilers
– hide block

In a word, yes. The Natau were not trying to kill her, but they did need something from her.

Quite enjoyed this, especially the visceral descriptions and that disturbing picture. I can honestly imagine what the holy structure looks like (I am glad I don’t have any idea what it smells like)

Quick question:

+ Spoilers?
- Close

When Dr. Beaumont is absorbed into the wall as the MTF found her, was the family trying to heal her wounds? Is that why she said not to extract her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The characterization in the logs overcomes this piece’s main weakness: by-the-numbers Sarkicism tropes that don’t get quite enough Cajun flair.

Also:

Dr. Beaumont: Ok, Manma. Let's talk about fishing perhaps?

I wanna know about the fishing, dang it! I think a story about two people going fishing and shooting the shit about their lives would go a long way towards making this setting stand on its own.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

The writing quality of this is overall very good. There's a few minor things, like that there's normally parentheses surrounding the mobile task force ("name"), and that the foetus in foetu would probably benefit from a footnote with a little explanation of what exactly it is.

Some lines in the description are great and really got me hooked right from the start. Things like the "birthing house" showed me what I could expect in the best way possible. I also feel like I got a lot of information about the group without being bored by too much text.

One extremely minor qualm I have with the notes from Isabelle is with the approximate area of 47 metres. Since 47 is prime, I struggle to see it coming out from an approximation of an area, and would probably change this to 50 honestly.

Oversight Committee

Huh, I've never heard of this. This isn't really a critique, more just me being interested about what exactly this is.

they use the babies to feed the kiraak

Missed a capitalisation for Kiraak here.

I'm a little unsure about the transcription of Delta's accent/dialect here, though it's not something intrusive enough to substantially affect my vote. I'm also a little unsure about the formatting of events etc. in the MTF Log, but that's another personal note (I prefer what I've used in SCP-4835. The log overall is great though. Dialogue is realistic and it includes that clinical description of gore that I love so much. I'm unsure about the blackbox though, am I missing something that would justify it?

Overall though, this is great. I was interested all the way through, even with my admittedly limited knowledge of Sarkicism (your crosslinking was great for me). I'd say that the weakest part is the personal log from the start, though I understand its necessity in the piece and it doesn't subtract from the whole. But yes, a great story with some incredible and horrific description and implications are totally enough to get my +1 here.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m pleased to have been able to assist you with another well-deserved success. I imagine you’ll be building up a fan base at this rate. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disturbing, disgusting, horrific, gross, nasty, ew, god, no, this is awful.

I love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, absolutely, this was a blast to read through! Sorry if that didn't come across my my original post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An additional iteration of SCP-4476-1A has been observed. It should also be noted that all six iterations are showing signs of pregnancy.

Noooooo

+1. Just… ick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will clean it in the morning, sure.

EDIT: Took me longer than I anticipated, but I've cleaned it up as you suggested.

Glad that you enjoyed(?) it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, that was… an experience. This hits a lot of my fears, from religion to backwater societies to births. It's a lot, even if I'm not well-verses in Sarkicism.

Would you consider including Dr. Beaumont's notes in a quoteblock or other div? It's a bit hard to figure out where her notes end and the document resumes.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Oh, there's lots of gross here. I was strongly inspired by the work that Metaphysician did on his Anthropological Approach to Sarkism pieces. If you haven't read them, I cannot give a stronger recommendation. Meta, your work continues to inspire and motivate. Thank you for sharing it with us.

This post wouldn't have happened without plaidypus, Doctor Fullham, Hatcheye, Francis Scalia. Thank you for putting up with my madness to help make this happen.

Finally, this is the first entry for a project that I'm working on with plaidypus on Le Rue Macabre and the denizens that find that place home. Look for more anomalous activity in the Bayou around New Orleans!

+ Licensing Info
- Hide Info
  • Map is from GoogleMaps, with slight edits by me.
  • Picture of the longhouse: https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/447cc156-c864-45f1-b887-aa7d6d15a23f
  • Picture of the patient with growth: https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/70ebba3d-c3b7-40bb-8435-344660f78c69
